samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, and green outputs, controlled by a pedestrian request button. The traffic light operates in a cycle of 60 clock cycles green, 5 clock cycles yellow, and 10 clock cycles red. If the pedestrian button is pressed while green, the green duration can be reduced to 10 clock cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian request signal.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">Count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Red light output.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow light output.</output>
        <output id="green">Green light output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1: Red light.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2: Yellow light.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3: Green light.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing.</reg>
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Current state of the traffic light controller.</reg>
        <reg id="p_red">Previous red signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_yellow">Previous yellow signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_green">Previous green signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if (pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if (!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if (!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the count value to the output clock
        assign clock = cnt;
        
        // Output logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                p_red <= red;
                p_yellow <= yellow;
                p_green <= green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
